item.title: CEB power outage – Saint Hubert, Grandport on 24 March 2026
item.description: Saint Hubert (Grandport): 09:00 to 16:00. Areas affected: Saint Hubert Village et Mare Chicose

item.description: LâUnion of Private Secondary Education Employees (UPSEE), en collaboration avec le ministÃ¨re de lâÃducation et la Swedish International Development Capacity Academy a lancÃ© un projet ...

item.title: Brent Crude Oil price – 21 March 2026
item.description: Brent Crude Oil: USD 112.19 per barrel

item.title: Bitcoin price – 21 March 2026
item.description: Bitcoin (BTC): USD 70,624.81

item.title: MUR exchange rates – 21 March 2026
item.description: MUR/USD 46.49 | MUR/EUR 53.72

item.title: Upcoming public holiday – Eid-Ul-Fitr
item.description: Today is Eid-Ul-Fitr

item.title: SEMDEX – 21 March 2026
item.description: SEMDEX closed at 2226.88

item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 21 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 11h30 on Saturday 21 March 2026.General situation:Few clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather at times.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Cloudy at times to the East and over the central plateau this afternoon with showers. Fair weather elsewhere.The maximum temperature will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 33 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Partly cloudy tonight and tomorrow morning with occasional showers over the central plateau.The minimum temperature will be between 19 and 21 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 23 and 25 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with waves of the order of 2 metres.High tides :14h31 and tomorrow 02h25.Low tides :20h42 and tomorrow 09h01.Sunset :18h20.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h14. Weather news for Mauritius issued at 11h30 on Saturday 21 March 2026.General situation:Few clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather at times.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Cloudy at times to the East and over the central plateau this afternoon with showers. Fair weather elsewhere.The maximum temperature will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 33 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Partly cloudy tonight and tomorrow morning with occasional showers over the central plateau.The minimum temperature will be between 19 and 21 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 23 and 25 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with waves of the order of 2 metres. About Us|Publications|Procurement|Survey Links|FAQ|Feedback|Contact Us|Useful Links © CopyrightMauritius Meteorological Services.

item.title: Africa’s top diamond producer Botswana shuts down U.S. base rumors at key air base
Africa’s biggest airline opens $50m terminal as it moves to handle travel surge
Ibrahim Traore's govt begins computer recycling to boost digital inclusion in Burkina Faso
Top 10 best airports in Africa for 2026 according to latest ranking
Energy leaders from Nigeria, 4 other African countries head to the US to address power sector challenges
How Helabet builds trust among the African bettors
The rise of ETH gambling sites and Africa’s expanding digital economy
Counder Conference 2026 brings African legends, OpenAI and 500 global leaders to Cape Town
Is the US a realistic career option for African professionals in 2026?
5 Tech leaders reveal biggest opportunities for women in Africa’s tech sector
How this product leader scaled one of the fastest-growing grocery platforms
Inside the lavish lifestyle of ex-Gambian president as probe links $362 million in public funds to jets, luxury cars and properties
French billionaire Bolloré to stand trial on corruption charges regarding African port deals
South Africa fights back against cheap steel imports from China and Thailand with up to 75% tariffs
After years of delays, Ghana enters global lithium race to help replace China in U.S. supply chains
Elon Musk misled Twitter investors in an effort to try to lower its purchase price, a jury finds
Top 10 African countries the United States made the most money last year
Africa’s largest university becomes first on the continent to own an Airport
US-Africa secret $40 million deportation deal turns scandalous as migrants are stranded in Equatorial Guinea
Guerre au Moyen-Orient : Maurice face au choc de sa dépendance alimentaire
Lutte contre le trafic de drogue et le blanchiment d’argent : Rs 9,9 milliards de biens sous le coup des saisies en 2025
Air Mauritius : deux vols directs supplémentaires vers Paris-CDG
Notation souveraine : Maurice met fin à son contrat avec S&P Global Ratings
Eid-ul-Fitr : entre rituels sacrés et joie partagée
Rajesh Bhagwan : «Ce sont les militants qui légitimisent notre présence au GM»
Vers un mini-remaniement
Bérenger quitte le gouvernement : rupture stratégique ou fracture politique?
Construction des logements sociaux Rs 515,2 M parties en fumée dans des contrats résiliés
Amédée Darga : «Le plus triste, c’est la dégringolade du MMM au fil des années»
À Rose-Hill : «Nu blese, me san Paul pena MMM, pena MMM si san Paul», confie un militant de longue date
Conférence sur la Gouvernance et le Leadership - Yoshinee Parasuraman : «Une organisation bien gouvernée est une organisation qui inspire la confiance»
Ce que Paul Bérenger reproche au gouvernement
Conférence sur la Gouvernance et le Leadership - Novan Maharahaje : «La gouvernance est le socle de la crédibilité d’une entreprise»
Ramgoolam « pas pressé » de nommer le remplaçant de Bérenger
Shaheen Abdul Carrim, AML/CFT : «La gouvernance n’est plus seulement une obligation réglementaire, mais un levier de crédibilité et de performance»
Shakeel Mohamed : «Je suis sûr que, dans l’intérêt du pays, Bérenger se montrera constructif. Je lui souhaite bonne continuation»
Conférence sur la Gouvernance et le Leadership : la gouvernance au service de la performance des entreprises
Après un accident de la route en septembre 2025 : la victime, Bhovinash Ropon, 35 ans, lance un appel de solidarité
L’Iran a ciblé la base de Diego Garcia avec des missiles balistiques
Iran says it will allow Japanese ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Japan sources 90 percent of its crude from Middle East and is heavily dependent on exports transiting the key waterway.

item.title: Trump hints at ‘winding down’ Iran war as US deploys more troops to region
item.description: Trump says he is not looking for a ceasefire with Iran as mixed messages from US president come amid an escalating war.

item.title: Eid-ul-Fitr : Célébrations à la Sunni Razvi Society en présence de plusieurs personnalités
item.description: La fête de l’Eid-ul-Fitr, qui marque la fin du mois sacré du Ramadan, est célébrée avec ferveur ce samedi 21 mars à la Sunni Razvi Academy à Port-Louis, à l’occasion de la 42ᵉ édition annuelle de l’Eid Milan Party organisée par la Sunni Razvi Society International. L’événement a rassemblé de nombreuses personnalités du pays dans une atmosphère de convivialité et de partage. La cérémonie officielle s’est tenue en présence du président de la République, Dharambe ...

item.title: Confidentiels : L'express leaks du dimanche 15 mars au vendredi 20 mars 2026
item.description: Plongez au cœur de l'actualité avec l'express, où nous vous dévoilons en exclusivité les derniers bruits de couloir, les buzz les plus récents et les scandales les plus retentissants que d'autres vous cachent. Restez informés et découvrez les dessous des événements qui façonnent notre société. **Assirvaden et les énergies renouvelables** ![patrick.jpg](https://backoffice.lexpress.mu/storage/TaDLRyIhjdu2msyHkREZUh8CMnK1PZGnQDuyrKIJ.jpg) Alors que le numéro deux du gouvern ...

item.title: Démission de Paul Bérenger comme Premier ministre adjoint : Une rupture qui se confirme au MMM
item.description: Rajesh Bhagwan, secrétaire général s’adressant à la presse, au nom de la direction du Mouvement militant mauricien (MMM), depuis le QG du parti à Port-Louis, a exprimé toute sa déception : *«Nou pe zwenn a enn moman bien bien tris du parti.»* Revenant sur le Comité central de mercredi dernier, il a rappelé les moments difficiles du MMM : *«Militan pandan 20 an inn sakrifie : 20 an mizer, 20 an soufrans !»* Rajesh Bhagwan s’est expliqué sur la raison pour laquelle il n’a p ...

item.title: Démission de Paul Bérenger : Le Premier ministre Navin Ramgoolam : «Les désaccords n’étaient pas majeurs»
item.description: La démission de Paul Bérenger comme Premier ministre adjoint continue à secouer la scène politique. Réagissant à cette décision, le Premier ministre Navin Ramgoolam a exprimé sa tristesse, hier soir, tout en affirmant avoir multiplié les efforts pour préserver l’alliance gouvernementale. Il s’est exprimé hier soir dans le JT de 19h30 de la Mauritius Broadcasting Corporation. *«Paul Bérenger a démissionné comme Premier ministre adjoint. C’est triste»*, a-t-il déclaré, ...

item.title: Tensions militaires : Deux missiles iraniens tirés vers Diego Garcia
item.description: L’Iran a tiré deux missiles balistiques vers la base américano-britannique de Diego Garcia, située en plein milieu de l’océan Indien, a rapporté vendredi le Wall Street Journal. Aucun des deux missiles tirés vers cette base à quelque 4 000 kilomètres du territoire iranien n’a touché sa cible, selon le journal, qui cite plusieurs responsables américains. L’un des missiles a connu une défaillance en vol et un navire de guerre américain a tiré un missile pour intercepter le s ...

item.title: En couverture : 🔔 Les grands titres de l'express de ce samedi 21 mars 2026 📰
item.description: En une de *l'express*: Démission comme DPM : Paul Bérenger retourn lor koltar ; Autre titre : Navin Ramgoolam : «Même lorsqu’il critiquait le gouvernement, je suis resté patient» l Rajesh Bhagwan : «Il a abandonné le MMM» l Ajay Gunness : «Li pa ti ena gang dan so biro li ?» l Premier League : Trois grands noms au bord de la relégation… l Éditorial : Zone de turbulences. **Démission comme DPM : Paul Bérenger retourn lor koltar** • Navin Ramgoolam : «Même lorsqu’il ...

item.title: 'Where is Eid?' For displaced people at Sudan's Tawila camp, holiday brings no relief
item.description: As Sudan celebrated the first day of Eid on Friday, displaced people at North Darfur’s Tawila camp gathered for prayers. But for them, the holiday brings no relief.

item.title: Eid-ul-Fitr : entre traditions, partage et émotions avec la famille Rohiman
item.description: Ce samedi 21 mars 2026 marque le point culminant d’un mois de dévotion spirituelle pour la communauté musulmane. À Maurice, l’Eid ul Fitr dépasse largement le cadre d’une simple célébration religieuse : elle symbolise la fin du mois sacré du Ramadan, période de jeûne, de discipline et de purification spirituelle, et ouvre la voie à un renouveau empreint de gratitude, de générosité et d’attachement aux valeurs religieuses et familiales. Il est à peine quatre heures du matin lorsque les premières

item.title: 'A bit of a mixed verdict': Musk misled Twitter shareholders, jury finds
item.description: Tech multi-billionaire Elon Musk could be forced to pay billions of dollars in damages after a federal jury found that the world's richest man had misled Twitter shareholders while purchasing the social media platform. Musk was found to have posted false statements to drive down the company's share price. FRANCE 24 correspondent Wassim Cornet reports from Los Angeles.

item.title: BTS return from military service - but does their album live up to the hype?
item.description: The band's 10th album sees them return to the rebellious, rap-heavy sound of their early days.

item.title: « Made in Moris en Fête » prévu à Bagatelle le 28 mars prochain
item.description: La première édition de « Made in Moris en Fête » se tiendra le samedi 28 mars au Bagatelle Mall. L’événement réunira plus de 40 marques locales, présentes dans les boutiques, le supermarché et les allées du centre commercial. Cette initiative vise à mettre en avant les produits labellisés Made in Moris et à encourager la consommation locale, en rapprochant les entreprises mauriciennes du public. Parmi les participants figurent notamment MCB Juice, Esko, Weshade, Miko, Blédor, Dina ainsi que la C

item.title: BTS comeback concert: Seoul gets ready to host 260,000 fans
item.description: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook will perform together for the first time since October 2022.

item.title: L’Iran lance des missiles en direction de Diego Garcia
item.description: L’Iran a lancé deux missiles balistiques en direction d’une base militaire conjointe américano britannique dans l’océan Indien vendredi, selon un rapport du Wall Street Journal. Les frappes n’ont pas atteint la base située à Diego Garcia, une petite île de l’archipel des Chagos, ont déclaré des responsables au journal. Un missile a échoué en vol et un navire de guerre américain aurait tiré un intercepteur SM 3 contre le second. On ne sait pas si cet intercepteur est responsable de l’échec du deu

item.title: Trump déclare que les États Unis envisagent de mettre progressivement fin à la guerre
item.description: Le président a fait cette déclaration sur les réseaux sociaux, peu après avoir indiqué aux journalistes, sur la pelouse de la Maison Blanche, qu’il n’était pas en faveur d’un cessez le feu, affirmant que les États Unis « anéantissaient l’autre camp ». Le département du Trésor a levé certaines sanctions sur le pétrole iranien. Le président Trump a écrit vendredi sur les réseaux sociaux que les États Unis envisageaient de « mettre progressivement fin » à la guerre avec l’Iran, estimant qu’ils étai

item.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient : pourquoi Donald Trump et Benjamin Netanyahu divergent sur l’Iran
item.description: Donald Trump n’est pas content. Mais cette fois, son ire n’est pas dirigée contre l’Iran, l’OTAN ou un pays européen. Le président américain en veut à Israël, son grand allié dans la guerre au Moyen-Orient. Il a fait savoir, jeudi 19 mars, qu’il n’avait pas apprécié la frappe israélienne la veille contre des champs gaziers iraniens, qui a entraîné des représailles iraniennes contre des infrastructures énergétiques au Qatar. Le président américain avait même affirmé, dans un premier temps, ne pas

item.title: Maguire, la catastrophe
item.description: Rappelé en sélection d’Angleterre ce vendredi, Harry Maguire a vécu une séquence catastrophique en milieu de la seconde période. Le défenseur central a d’abord provoqué un but contre son camp de James Hill, permettant aux Red Devils de prendre l’avantage (71e), puis a bêtement accroché Evanilson dans sa surface, ce qui lui a valu un carton rouge (78e). C’est Éli Junior Kroupi qui a transformé le penalty du 2-2 à un quart d’heure de la fin, pour son 9e but de la saison. United compte quatre point

item.title: Titre de la CAN 2025 attribué au Maroc : quelles ont été les autres victoires sur tapis vert ?
item.description: Dans un énième coup de théâtre, deux mois après une finale qui s’était terminée dans le chaos, le jury d’appel de la Confédération africaine de football (CAF) a retiré, mardi 17 mars, le titre remporté par le Sénégal lors de la Coupe d’Afrique des nations (CAN) et l’a attribué au Maroc. Il s’agit d’une première pour une finale d’une compétition continentale de ce niveau. Par le passé, des équipes ont toutefois déjà obtenu des victoires sur tapis vert à la suite de décisions purement administrati

item.title: Streamlining the kill chain: how AI is changing modern warfare
item.description: The US-Israeli war with Iran has put AI use in defence systems in the spotlight like never before. AI's exact role in the military, its accuracy and possible repercussions are slowly beginning to emerge – but the extent of its use and role in the war so far still largely remain a mystery.

item.title: Des courses automobiles envisagées au stade Anjalay Coopen
item.description: Le gouvernement envisage d’organiser des événements de sport automobile sur le parking du stade Anjalay Coopen, à la suite d’une visite effectuée sur le site par le ministre de la jeunesse et des sports, Deven Nagalingum, et le ministre du transport terrestre, Osman Mahomed. Cette visite avait pour objectif d’évaluer les travaux de réaménagement nécessaires afin de permettre la tenue de compétitions dans des conditions sécurisées. Les autorités souhaitent offrir aux passionnés de vitesse un espa

item.title: Middle East war live: Trump mulls 'winding down' Iran war as US sends thousands more marines
item.description: US President Donald Trump suggested Friday he was considering “winding down” military operations against Iran even as US media reported Washington was sending three more amphibious assault ships and roughly 2,500 additional marines to the region. Israel continued to hit Tehran and Beirut with air strikes overnight as the US-Israeli war against Iran stretched into its fourth week. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

item.title: LIVE: Japan vs Australia – Women’s Asian Cup 2026 final
item.description: Follow our live build-up, with team news, before our text commentary stream of the Women's Asian Cup final.

item.title: Maurice accueille une conférence internationale sur l’enseignement transnational
item.description: Maurice accueillera les 25 et 26 mars une conférence internationale dédiée à l’enseignement transnational (TNE), organisée par l’Uniciti International Education Hub (UIEH) en partenariat avec Times Higher Education. L’événement réunira experts, universitaires et décideurs afin d’examiner les évolutions du TNE et son rôle dans le développement de l’enseignement supérieur à l’échelle mondiale. La conférence abordera plusieurs thématiques, notamment l’innovation, les partenariats internationaux, la

item.title: Météo : beau temps en général sur l’île
item.description: Un vent léger à modéré continue d’influencer les conditions météorologiques sur l’île Maurice, avec quelques averses attendues principalement à l’est et sur le plateau central en début de journée. Selon les prévisions, le temps sera généralement beau au cours de la journée, avant de devenir mi- couvert durant la nuit, avec des ondées occasionnelles sur les hauteurs. Les températures maximales varieront entre 26 et 29 °C sur les terrains élevés et entre 31 et 34 °C sur les régions côtières. Les m

item.title: Alliance Remains Intact Despite Bérenger’s Resignation, Declares PM Ramgoolam
item.description: Prime Minister Navin Ramgoolam has moved to reassure the nation that the “Alliance du Changement” remains stable, despite the high-profile resignation of Deputy Prime Minister Paul Bérenger. In a televised address broadcast on the national station this evening, the … The post Alliance Remains Intact Despite Bérenger’s Resignation, Declares PM Ramgoolam appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: Vol avec violence à Port-Louis : Deux suspects arrêtés
item.description: Un homme a été victime d’un vol avec violence aux environs de Line Barracks, à Port-Louis. Il a été agressé par trois individus. Selon les premiers éléments de l’enquête, les suspects lui ont demandé une cigarette avant de s’en prendre à lui devant son refus. Ils lui ont dérobé son téléphone portable ainsi que Rs 2 000 avant de prendre la fuite. Blessée lors de l’agression, la victime a reçu des soins. Deux suspects ont été interpellés. L’enquête se poursuit. The post Vol avec violence à Port-Lo

item.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer actor Nicholas Brendon dies aged 54
item.description: Brendon died of "natural cases", his family said in a statement, remembering him as "passionate" and "sensitive".

item.title: Iran Fires 2 Ballistic Missiles at Diego Garcia in First Long-Range Strike
item.description: Iran has launched two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, marking a significant escalation and the first operational use of such weaponry to target US and British interests outside the Middle East. According to US officials, the joint military … The post Iran Fires 2 Ballistic Missiles at Diego Garcia in First Long-Range Strike appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: PwC Mauritius Among 3 Subsidiaries Blacklisted by World Bank over Fraud
item.description: The World Bank Group has announced a 21-month suspension of three PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) entities following an investigation into anti-competitive and fraudulent practices. The debarment affects Mauritius-based PwC Associates Africa Ltd, along with PricewaterhouseCoopers Limited in Kenya and PricewaterhouseCoopers Rwanda … The post PwC Mauritius Among 3 Subsidiaries Blacklisted by World Bank over Fraud appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: 300 million people celebrate Nowruz under a cloud of war
item.description: Millions of people have rung in the ancient Persian New Year, Nowruz, as war grips the Middle East.

item.title: Aid flotilla for Cuba sets sail from Mexico
item.description: An aid flotilla from Mexico is sailing for Cuba as the country suffers under a tightening US fuel blockade.

item.title: Russia's school propaganda was highlighted by Oscar-winning film - but does it work?
item.description: The messages are clear: the full-scale invasion is a defensive war and patriotism means unquestionable loyalty.

item.title: Socialists battle to hold Paris in key mayoral elections across France
item.description: Emmanuel Grégoire is facing a challenge from Rachida Dati on the right who is behind in the opinion polls.

item.title: Remember Chuck Norris memes but never watched his films? You're not alone
item.description: Chuck Norris became an inadvertent source of humour for a viral online celebration of his movie tough-guy persona.

item.title: Al Jazeera reporter’s journey from Jordan to Iraq
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Nicolas Haque recorded his journey on the long road from Amman to Baghdad.

item.title: Africa: Sun Africa, Exim, African Ministers Set Project-Focused Tone At Powering Africa Summit
item.description: [Premium Times] Washington, DC -- Uganda's Minister of Energy & Mineral Development, Ruth Ssentamu, stressed that energy access remains central to economic transformation.

item.title: The fight to control the narrative in the Afghan-Pakistan conflict
item.description: The battle between the neighbouring countries is taking place as much online as it is on the ground.

item.title: Elon Musk misled shareholders during Twitter purchase, US jury finds
item.description: Tech multi-billionaire Elon Musk could be forced to pay billions of dollars in damages after a federal jury found that the world's richest man had misled Twitter shareholders while purchasing the social media platform by posting false statements to drive down the company's share price. The tycoon's lawyers said that Musk would appeal the decision.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump says no ceasefire as Khamenei issues defiant message
item.description: Trump says Strait of Hormuz must be protected from Iranian attacks 'by other nations who use it'.

item.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as gas prices soar
item.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

item.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors, jury finds
item.description: A San Francisco jury concludes Musk’s claims in 2022 were damaging to a group of Twitter investors.

item.title: US judge sides with New York Times against Pentagon journalism policies
item.description: In his ruling, Judge Paul Friedman ruled the Trump administration sought to force out 'disfavoured journalists'.

item.title: Google Translate exposes Russian spy unit
item.description: Have we just seen the most embarrassing intelligence failure Russia has suffered in years? Investigative group The Insider revealed how a secret Russian unit - known as Center 795 - allegedly ran assassinations and covert operations abroad, but was exposed in a remarkably simple way: one of its agents used Google Translate. According to the investigation, that allowed the FBI to read their communications in real time. So how did a supposedly “untraceable” elite unit unravel so easily, and what d

item.title: Pezeshkian says Iran not seeking war with Muslim neighbours
item.description: Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian says his country is not seeking war with its neighbours.

item.title: Colombia’s President Gustavo Petro under investigation in US for drug ties
item.description: Faced with reports that two US prosecutors have opened probes, the left-wing leader denies links to drug traffickers.

item.title: As Islamophobia rises, Australia's Muslims celebrate Eid
item.description: Recent events abroad and at home have fuelled an ongoing surge of Islamophobia in Australia.

item.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
item.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

item.title: Iran strikes gulf oil, gas sites as war jolts energy markets, what cost for South Africa ?
item.description: As tensions continue in the Middle East, the economic fallout is being felt far beyond the region. In South Africa, officials are warning that the ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel and Iran could come at a steep cost. Our South African correspondent speaks to political analyst Sanusha Naidu about the economic effect the war has on South Africa and the greater continent.

item.title: US prosecutors investigate Colombia’s Petro over suspected ties to drug traffickers
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro is being examined in criminal investigations by two US federal prosecutors' offices, according to a New York Times report published on Friday. Relations between Washington and Bogota entered a volatile phase after US President Donald Trump's return to office 14 months ago, and the two leaders have clashed over various issues.

item.title: India cooking gas crisis forces exodus of textile workers
item.description: Indian textile workers are returning home after days without cooking gas, as an LPG crisis linked to the Iran war.

item.title: Uganda: Rhinos return to Kidepo Valley Park for first time since 1983
item.description: Ugandan wildlife authorities have reintroduced rhinos into a remote protected area where they were once poached into extinction, an event seen by conservationists as a milestone in efforts to support the recovery of a species threatened by poaching.

item.title: Military movements indicate Trump is considering Iran ground operation
item.description: The US is moving military assets to the Middle East that are key to providing support for ground troop operations.

item.title: Palestinian citizens of Israel demand better protection against missiles
item.description: At least 15 people have been killed by attacks in Israel since the war on Iran started late last month.

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei says enemy ‘defeated’ in message
item.description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has released a message for the Persian New Year.

item.title: Ex-minister Gamboa targeted in Costa Rica’s first extradition to the US
item.description: A former Supreme Court justice, Celso Gamboa was accused by the US of using government connections to traffic cocaine.

item.title: US says it struck alleged drug trafficking vessel in Pacific, killing two
item.description: In an initial statement, the US military said three people survived the strike but did not offer additional details.

item.title: In Sudan's refugee camps, Eid is overshadowed by war
item.description: Displaced Sudanese citizens gathered for Eid prayers and preparec food in Tawila camp in North Darfur, as the war in their home country weighs heavily on the celebrations.

item.title: Ukraine-Hungary oil pipeline row threatens EU loan
item.description: Kyiv and Budapest disagree on how badly damaged a hub in western Ukraine bombed by Russia really is, as its oil flow remains suspended.

item.title: Will Europe be pulled into the Iran war?
item.description: Europe is under pressure as Trump pushes allies to support the Iran war. How are they responding?

item.title: What are the risks of turning energy sites into battlefields?
item.description: Oil and gas facilities in the Gulf have been attacked since early in the war on Iran.

item.title: Iran: The ‘Mosaic Doctrine’, Zelensky V Orban, Hungary-splaining
item.description: In a week where assassinations have continued within the top ranks of Iran’s regime, the minister of intelligence, the commander of the Bases paramilitary and the powerful security chief Ali Larijani included, but a weakened government seems to have had little bearing on Tehran’s retaliation across Israel and nine gulf states.Just one week ago, President Trump spoke of a war that was, “very complete, pretty much”, that now appears to have been premature, and over-confident. There’s no more talk

item.title: QatarEnergy CEO says warned US, industry officials against attack on energy
item.description: QatarEnergy CEO Saad al-Kaabi says he warned US of 'consequences' of attacking Iranian energy infrastructure.

item.title: A spiralling energy crisis: Strikes on gas, oil sites have sent prices soaring again
item.description: An analysis of the spiralling energy crisis with Mohamad Al-Sabban, energy economist and longtime advisor to the Saudi Government.

item.title: Trump: No Iranian leaders left to talk to, says war going ‘extremely well’
item.description: US President Donald Trump said that Iranian leaders are “all gone” and that the White House has “nobody left to talk to.

item.title: Sri Lanka says it denied US request to land two aircraft at Mattala airport
item.description: President Anura Kumara Dissanayake says request made on February 26, two days before US and Israel launched war on Iran.

item.title: Walker, Texas Ranger star and martial arts icon Chuck Norris dies at 86
item.description: A six-time karate champion and action film star, Norris was hospitalised in Hawaii on Thursday for unknown causes.

item.title: Iranians celebrate Persian New Year in first wartime Nowruz in decades
item.description: Residents of Tehran exchange new year's wishes as US and Israeli attacks continue through the spring equinox.

item.title: Iran: From Khamenei to Khamenei
item.description: How Iran’s power structure was built, and how it survives its architect.

item.title: Trump administration seeks billions from Harvard in anti-Semitism lawsuit
item.description: Harvard University has rejected the complaint as 'another pretextual and retaliatory action' from the Trump White House.

item.title: Rapport de l’Audit 2024-25 : Â«Â Seulement 41 % des recommandations de l’annÃ©e prÃ©cÃ©dente appliquÃ©esÂ Â»
item.description: Plus de 720 recommandations, soit presque le double des 363 recommandations de l'exercice prÃ©cÃ©dent. Câest par ce chiffre sans prÃ©cÃ©dent que le Directeur de l'Audit, le Dr D. Paligadu, marque la publication du rapport annuel du National Audit Office...

item.title: No, Iran didn't publish a 'list' of 11 American cities to target
item.description: A video claiming that Iran published a list of 11 American cities it plans to target has gone viral since it was first posted on Instagram on March 3. We found the origin of this list, and it turns out that it has nothing to do with Iran.

item.title: 'Polish populists have decided to turn anti-European': Warsaw mayor Rafal Trzaskowski
item.description: Talking Europe travels to Warsaw to meet the mayor of the city and former presidential candidate Rafal Trzaskowski, just as the political temperature rises ahead of high-stakes parliamentary elections scheduled for next year. Trzaskowski argues that a long-standing consensus in Polish politics – being pro-European and pro-Ukraine – has been broken, as populist forces try to outdo each other in an effort to attract voters. Trzaskowski is an ally of Prime Minister Donald Tusk and was Civic Coaliti

item.title: Liverpool condemns 'dehumanising, cowardly' racist abuse of Ibrahima Konate
item.description: Liverpool defender Ibrahima Konate was the target of “vile and abhorrent” racist abuse online, the Premier League club said Friday.

item.title: Action film star Chuck Norris dies, aged 86
item.description: He was the star, taciturn lawman of the show "Walker, Texas Ranger": Chuck #Norris passed away aged 86 on March 19. The martials arts grandmaster turned actor had become the iconic, tough guy over the years.

item.title: Flights, petrol cars and cruise ships: Amsterdam bans fossil fuel ads
item.description: Cities around the world are clearing their billboards of ads for flights, cruise ships and petrol cars in an effort to reduce planet-warming gases. Amsterdam is the latest city to join the movement, becoming the first capital in the world to approve a legal ban on fossil fuel advertising. Dutch activists are now calling for restrictions to be enforced on a national level. Our Down to Earth team reports.

item.title: Rescued cheetahs find refuge in Somaliland amid trafficking crisis
item.description: Inside a guarded sanctuary on the savannah, 127 rescued cheetahs pace, stretch and play — survivors of a brutal illegal trade that is pushing the species toward the brink.

item.title: Alliance of Sahel states outraged at EU demand to free Niger's president
item.description: The European parliament voted last week to pass a resolution demanding the "immediate" release of Niger's former president Mohamed Bazoum, who was ousted in a coup in 2023 and has been detained ever since. The Alliance of Sahel states reacted with indignation.

item.title: Africa: How the World's Muslims Celebrate Eid 2026?
item.description: [ENA] As the sun rose over a Middle East scarred by weeks of relentless conflict, the call to prayer resonated amidst the distant thunder of the front lines. Yet the region did not retreat into silence. Instead, it responded with a defiant, multi-colored symphony of tradition. Since the opening salvos weeks ago, a war many feared. But few truly expected with such speed, has reshaped the emotional and economic landscape of the region.

item.title: Myth of liberation: As war rages, trapped Iranians 'curse' the regime, the Americans & the Israelis
item.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Borzou Daragahi, award-winning international correspondent and investigative reporter. As Iran marks Nowruz, the Persian New Year, war intrudes into civilian spaces. The psychological terrain is marked less by overt anger than by fatigue, resignation, and a deepening sense of entrapment. A media blackout and communication breakdowns intensify this isolation, eroding social cohesion and amplifying uncertainty. External narratives of “liberation” fail to r

item.title: Kenya: Kenyan Sprinter Hopes to Leave World Indoor Championships a Superstar
item.description: [Capital FM] NAIROBI, Kenya, March 20, 2026 - Kenyan sprinter Mercy Oketch hopes to make an indelible mark at the ongoing World Indoor Championships in Kujawy Pormoze, Poland.

item.title: Africa: How Morocco Is Redrawing Africa's Avocado Trade Map
item.description: [Independent (Kampala)] Africa exported roughly 430,000 tonnes of avocados in 2025, but the balance of power moved north. Preliminary 2025 data show a decisive shift in volumes, logistics strategy, and market access, signalling how infrastructure and route stability now matter as much as farm output.

item.title: Israel says struck Syrian army camps after Druze 'attacked'
item.description: Israel's military said Friday it had struck Syrian army camps in response to what it called attacks against the Druze community in the southern Sweida province, with the Syrian foreign ministry calling it "an outrageous assault".

item.title: 'No obvious short-term way out' of war, French top diplomat tells Israel
item.description: Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said on Friday he did not see an obvious end to ​the conflict in the Middle East in the short-term, but that France and its allies would continue to work towards trying to find a lasting solution. "There is no obvious short-term ​way out ‌of the ongoing regional escalation, which has in some ways been ⁠unfolding since October 7, 2023. But this must in no way serve as a pretext for inaction," Barrot told reporters ‌after meeting with Israeli counterpart Gideon Sa

item.title: Zimbabwe: Senator Challenges Impact of Global Wars On Zimbabwe Fuel Costs
item.description: [263Chat] Zanu PF's Uzumba Maramba Pfungwe (UMP) Senator Jerry Gotora has questioned why fuel prices remain high in the country, arguing that existing local production and infrastructure should be helping to keep costs down.

item.title: South Africa: Decline in Road Crash Stats
item.description: [SAnews.gov.za] Government is making progress in reducing road accidents, with the latest preliminary data for the period 1 January to 15 March showing an 11% decline in crashes compared to the same period in 2025.

item.title: Nasa's Artemis Moon rocket rolls back to pad for possible April launch
item.description: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

item.title: Mozambique: PM Swears Senior Officials Into Office
item.description: [AIM] Maputo -- Mozambican Prime Minister Benvinda Levi on Thursday swore into office four senior officials, all of whom are women.

item.title: Nigeria: Ex-Rep Member Dumps PDP for ADC in Cross River
item.description: [Premium Times] The News Agency of Nigeria reports that multiple defections to the ADC are gradually reshaping Cross River's political landscape ahead of the 2027 general elections.

item.title: Iran threatens to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide
item.description: Iran threatened to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide and insisted it was still building missiles

item.title: Iran's Khamenei says enemy 'defeated' in Nowruz message
item.description: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei on Friday said the enemies of the Islamic republic were being defeated in the war against the US and Israel in a written message for the Persian New Year, Nowruz. The message was read out on Iranian television, with Khamenei yet to appear publicly since the beginning of the war.

item.title: 'Trump could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment,' ex-adviser John Bolton says
item.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, former US national security adviser John Bolton said US President Donald Trump "could be ready to stop the war at almost any moment". Bolton also said he remains mystified by Trump's reversal on regime change in Iran and warned that the Iranian opposition is not receiving enough support.

item.title: Rose-Hill : De la drogue découverte après un accident de la route
item.description: Un accident de la route survenu à Rose-Hill a conduit à la découverte d’une substance suspectée d’être de la drogue. L’accident, impliquant une voiture et un scooter, a fait deux blessés, le conducteur du deux-roues et son passager. Ils ont été transportés à l’hôpital. Selon les premiers éléments de l’enquête, le conducteur de la voiture présentait des signes laissant penser qu’il était sous l’influence de substances illicites. Une fouille du véhicule a permis la saisie de cinq feuilles d’alumin

item.title: Israel strikes Syria after Druze clashes
item.description: Syria condemned the strikes on weapons sites and government infrastructure as an "outrageous" assault on Syria's sovereignty.

item.title: New BTS album drops as Seoul gets ready for comeback mega-gig
item.description: K-pop megastars BTS released a new album Friday ahead of their open-air comeback concert in Seoul, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people. It will be BTS's first concert after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service.

item.title: 'Harm at a population level': World Happiness Report flags social media's negative impact
item.description: Young people have a “much lower” level of wellbeing than 15 years ago due to the effects of social media use, according to the 2026 edition of the World Happiness Report, which sees Finland maintain its position as the world’s happiest country for the ninth consecutive year.

item.title: Finland is world's happiest country ninth year in a row, UN happiness report finds
item.description: Finland maintains its top position as the world's happiest country for the ninth year running, as it scores 7.764 on a 10-point scale, according to an annual UN-sponsored report. Known for its nature, sauna culture and extensive welfare system, the country's 5.6 million inhabitants generally report a high level of trust in authorities and low levels of inequality.

item.title: Dana Lixenberg's personal, political and pared-down portraits
item.description: Her photographs of celebrated personalities, from Toni Morrison to Tupac Shakur, have become iconic images. Dana Lixenberg tells us about three decades of providing her unique perspective on the United States and the evolution of a society she first started documenting in the 1990s. As her major, monographic exhibition "American Images" opens at the Maison Européenne de la Photo in Paris, the Dutch photographer tells us about being surprised by the enduring success of her beautiful black-and-whi

item.title: Sudanese artists rebuild cultural life in wartime shelter
item.description: In a school-turned-shelter on the Red Sea coast, music rises above the hardships of war as displaced Sudanese artists recreate a sense of community and purpose.

item.title: 🔴 Live: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei issues defiant statement on Persian New Year as blasts rock Tehran
Despite decapitation, Iran’s regime endures: Does Hormuz blockade give Tehran the upper hand?
item.description: William Hilderbrandt is pleased to welcome Paul Taylor, Senior Visiting Fellow in the Europe in the World Programme and a member of the Defence/Security EUrope project. He is also a freelance columnist for The Guardian. Amid the Strait of Hormuz blockade, Mr. Taylor argues that even overwhelming force cannot guarantee safety when asymmetric tools, such as drones and decentralised command structures, remain effective. At its core, he contends that reopening the Strait is not a technical or logist

item.title: Norway's crown princess breaks silence, claiming she was 'manipulated and deceived' by Epstein
item.description: Crown Princess Mette Marit tells Norwegian TV she wishes she had never met the late sex offender.

item.title: Tanzania: TMA Issues an Alert Warning of Heavy Rainfall Across the Tanzanian Regions
item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has issued a weather alert warning of heavy rainfall across multiple regions, urging residents to take appropriate precautions to avoid potential hazards caused by adverse weather conditions.

item.title: New WTO report: high oil, gas prices serious challenge for trade growth
item.description: The World Trade Organization warned Thursday that prolonged high oil and gas prices in 2026 would "shave off" growth from an already grim trade forecast.

item.title: Nigerian Muslims mark End of Ramadan with Eid al-Fitr prayers
item.description: Muslims in Nigeria and around the world marked the end of the fasting month of Ramadan and ushered in the holiday of Eid al-Fitr on Friday with prayers.

item.title: Namibia: Gondwana - Miss Universe 1992 Returns to Namibia
item.description: [Namibian] More than three decades after Michelle McLean was crowned Miss Universe, she is finally welcoming the friends who shared that moment with her - not on stage, but on a journey across her home country.

item.title: Namibia: Five People Die in Car Crash Between Okahandja and Otjiwarongo, One Survivor
item.description: [Namibian] Five people died in a car crash between Okahandja and Otjiwarongo on Thursday evening at 18h00.

item.title: ConsÃ©cration -Communications et relations publiques : Coup double consÃ©cration pour Blast Burson avec Diamond et African SABRE...
item.description: Blast Burson franchit une Ã©tape historique. Lâagence mauricienne a dÃ©crochÃ© son tout premier Diamond SABRE Award, ainsi quâun 12e African SABRE Award, prix prestigieux dans lâuniver...

item.title: Burkina Faso’s tomato export ban raises concerns over regional supply
item.description: Burkina Faso’s decision to ban the export of fresh tomatoes is drawing attention across West Africa, with traders and consumers watching closely for possible effects on supply and prices.

item.title: Africa: Iran War Constricting International Travel
item.description: [This Day] The war between Iran, the United States and Israel is disrupting international travels, especially in the Gulf region; causing major Gulf carriers like Emirates, Qatar Airways, Etihad to lose about $200 million daily, according to the latest report by simple.flying, a renowned online aviation magazine.

item.title: Senegal/Liberia: George Weah Faults CAF On Decision to Strip Senegal
item.description: [This Day] Former Ballon d'Or winner and three-time African Player of the Year, George Manneh Weah, Sr, has faulted CAF's decision to strip Senegal the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations the Teranga Lions won in Rabat last January.

item.title: Alpinisme – Mauricien Ã lâÃ©preuve : Jaysen Arumugum vise les Seven Summits
item.description: Originaire de Moka, Jaysen Arumugum sâapprÃªte Ã relever lâun des dÃ©fis les plus extrÃªmes et isolÃ©s de la planÃ¨te : lâascension du Mont Vinson, le plus haut sommet de lâAntarcti...

item.title: Nowruz in Tehran: Crowded markets dispte ongoing airstrikes
item.description: Iran’s capital Tehran showed signs of cautious activity on 19 March 2026 as residents prepared for Nowruz, despite ongoing US-Israeli strikes that have disrupted daily life since 28 February.

item.title: Cameroon delays parliamentary elections, lawmakers to stay until end 2026
item.description: Cameroonian lawmakers yet again voted on Thursday to extend their mandate, according to official media.

item.title: Nowruz in London: Iranian diaspora marks spring amid grief over war in Iran
item.description: Members of the Iranian diaspora in north London gathered on 20 March to mark Nowruz, the Persian New Year, despite concern over the ongoing war in Iran.

item.title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
item.description: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

item.title: Madagascar’s president orders polygraph for aspiring ministers
item.description: Madagascar's military leader, President Michael Randrianirina, announced Thursday that anyone applying to become a minister in the new government will undergo lie detector tests to screen for corruption—a rare and unprecedented vetting measure.

item.title: West Africa: Nigeria, UK Seal £746m Deal to Rebuild Apapa, Tin Can Ports
item.description: [Leadership] Nigeria has secured a £746 million export finance agreement with the United Kingdom to support the redevelopment of Lagos' Apapa and Tin Can Island port complexes.

item.title: Kenya: Kenya's Finance Minister Defends Infrastructure Fund but Includes Misleading Claims
item.description: [Africa Check] Kenya's finance minister defends infrastructure fund but includes misleading claims

item.title: Nigeria: Beyond the Numbers - What Data and Community Voices Reveal About Maternal Deaths in Nigeria
item.description: [Nigeria Health Watch] Oladimeji Solomon Yemi and Safiya Shuaibu Isa (Lead writers)

item.title: Kenya: Partnerships Drive Push for Digital Health Growth At Smart Summit 2026
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Industry leaders, government officials, and innovators have called for deeper collaboration to unlock the full potential of digital health systems, as partnerships take center stage at the fifth edition of the Smart Summit 2026.

item.title: Africa: Haysom's Life 'Marked by an Unwavering Commitment to Justice, Peace, and Constitutionalism'
item.description: [allAfrica] Geneva -- The UN Commission on Human Rights in South Sudan has expressed its profound sorrow at the passing of Nicholas ''Fink'' Haysom, Special Representative of the Secretary-General and Head of the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS).

item.title: Egyptians attend prayers at a mosque to mark Eid celebrations
item.description: Egyptians attended prayers at a mosque in Cairo on Friday to mark the holiday of Eid al-Fitr, and bid farewell to the to the Islamic holy month of Ramadan.

item.title: Nigeria: Shettima Present As Borno Witnesses Peaceful Eid Prayers Amid Tight Security
item.description: [Vanguard] The 2026 Eid el-Fitr sallah festival was observed peacefully across Maiduguri, the Borno State capital, despite the fear over the renewed bomb explosions and attacks on residents and military formations across the state.

item.title: Gold Prices Plunge 17% as Global Market Shifts Impact Local Trade
item.description: The price of gold in Mauritius has plummeted by 17% since mid-February, marking a significant correction in the precious metals market. Local importers report that while the downward trend has been developing for weeks, the momentum has accelerated sharply, with … The post Gold Prices Plunge 17% as Global Market Shifts Impact Local Trade appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: South Africa: High Court Urged to Permit Damages Claims Against Agrochemicals Giant Upl
item.description: [Daily Maverick] A group of Durban residents has urged the KwaZulu-Natal Division of the High Court to authorise a class action lawsuit against the Indian agrochemicals giant UPL for health injuries and financial damage they allegedly suffered after a toxic pesticide inferno.

item.title: Gulf crisis draws attention to African oil [Business Africa]
item.description: Unlike crude from the middle east, a lot of African oil does not have to transit vulnerable corridors such as the Strait of Hormuz, where events like war can put a stop to shipping. But years of constrained investment meant African producers cannot pump more and fast enough to cover supply gaps

item.title: UK and Nigeria agree deal to speed up deportations
item.description: Nigeria and the United Kingdom have signed a new agreement aimed at accelerating the return of Nigerians without legal status in Britain, including failed asylum seekers, visa overstayers and convicted offenders.

item.title: Curepipe Targets 500 Abandoned Plots of Land to Halt Rising Rat Infestation
item.description: Authorities in Curepipe have launched a major offensive against a growing rat infestation as the town emerges alongside Port Louis as one of the areas most affected by the pests. The escalation comes in response to a rise in leptospirosis … The post Curepipe Targets 500 Abandoned Plots of Land to Halt Rising Rat Infestation appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: Tunisian anti-racism activist sentenced to eight years in prison
item.description: A Tunisian court on Thursday sentenced anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah to eight years in prison on financial misconduct charges, a ruling her lawyer condemned as "shocking" and politically motivated, marking the latest crackdown on civil society in the North African country.

item.title: Tanzania: Tanzania Network Pushes to Raise Legal Marriage Age for Girls to 18
item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- THE Tanzania Ending Child Marriage Network has pledged to continue advocating for policy reforms to ensure the legal age of marriage for girls is raised to 18 years.

item.title: Nigeria: Borno Governor Warns of Possible Suicide Attacks in Maiduguri During Eid
item.description: [Premium Times] Governor Babagana Zulum disclosed this in an interview with the BBC Hausa Service, monitored in Damaturu, on Friday

item.title: Africa: Afreximbank Urges Acceleration of Implementation of the Single Continental Market With AfCFTA Adjustment Fund
item.description: [Afreximbank] On 10 March, the AfCFTA Adjustment Fund Corporation organised a stakeholders' engagement event in Lagos to introduce the AfCFTA Adjustment Fund to Nigerian institutional investors as a catalytic instrument supporting the implementation of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA).

item.title: NGOs in DR Congo offer street children skills and hope of a better future
item.description: Several thousand children are estimated to live on the streets in the Congolese capital, Kinshasa.

item.title: French tycoon Vincent Bolloré to stand trial in ‘African ports’ corruption case
item.description: French industrialist Vincent Bolloré will stand trial in December before the Paris criminal court in the so‑called “African ports” case.

item.title: M-PESA to stop sharing full phone numbers with merchants, banks by year-end
item.description: Safaricom, Kenya’s largest telecoms operator, plans to extend data minimisation across its mobile money service M-PESA by late 2026, expanding controls that limit the exposure of customer phone numbers in mobile money transactions.

item.title: South Africa's traditional circumcision claims dozens of young lives
item.description: At least 48 boys and young men died during recent initiation ceremonies in South Africa, among them 22-year-old Lamkelo Mtyho, who had no known health issues when he entered a registered school—but never returned.

item.title: South Africa: Fuel Runs Dry in Nelson Mandela Bay
item.description: [Scrolla] Petroleum companies have started limiting fuel in Nelson Mandela Bay, blaming the Gulf war for putting local supplies at risk. Oil companies are now charging surcharges on normal prices, from 5c per litre for petrol to around R10 per litre for paraffin.

item.title: Kenya: Nyongo Rallies Kisumu Residents Ahead of Ruto-Museveni SGR Launch
item.description: [Capital FM] Kisumu -- Kisumu Governor Prof. Anyang' Nyong'o has called on residents to turn-out in large numbers for the forthcoming visit by President William Ruto to launch the extension of the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Naivasha to Kisumu.

item.title: Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM
item.description: President Trump compared US strikes on Iran to the deadly 1941 Japanese attack and declaration of war as part of World War Two.

item.title: Tributes pour in from across the world for South African peace advocate
item.description: Anti-apartheid activist, human rights campaigner, and constitutional lawyer, Nicholas “Fink” Haysom, died on Wednesday aged 73.

item.title: Tunisia: Tunisian Independence - Between Political Negotiations and Armed Resistance
item.description: [Tunis Afrique Presse] Tunis -- March 20, 1956 was not an ordinary date, but a decisive milestone in Tunisia's contemporary history. On that day, after 75 years of French colonial domination, Tunisia broke with a past of subjugation, following the signing in Paris of the independence protocol.

item.title: Liberia: Boakai, Senators Tight-Lipped On Conference Amid Insecurity At Liberia-Guinea Border
item.description: [FrontPageAfrica] Monrovia -- President Joseph Boakai and members of the Liberian Senate are maintaining silence over the outcome of a crucial closed door meeting held at the Capitol Building in Monrovia, as unease continues to grow over mounting tensions along the border between Liberia and Guinea.

item.title: Africa: After Centuries, TB Is Still the Bridesmaid, Never the Bride
item.description: [spotlight] The wins are there but the underlying reality remains, writes Professor Kogie Naidoo as she likens TB to a bridesmaid awaiting its turn to garner attention. The analogy is relevant given the overwhelming number of new TB infections and deaths each year globally for a disease persistently sitting in the shadow of other communicable and non-communicable diseases.

item.title: Morocco/Senegal: CAF Faces Pressure As Guinea Contests Morocco's 1976 Afcon Win
item.description: [Vanguard] The Guinea Football Federation has urged the Confederation of African Football (CAF) to review the 1976 AFCON outcome, citing Morocco's walkout and a recent ruling against Senegal.

item.title: Kenya: Kenya On Alert As Us-Iran-Israel Tensions Heighten Global Risks - CS Tuya
item.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Defence Cabinet Secretary Soipan Tuya has assured Kenyans that the country remains vigilant in the face of evolving global security threats, including developments linked to US- Iran - Israel conflict and wider Middle East instability.

item.title: Rwanda: Rwanda Expands Surgical Access with New Kibungo Teaching Hospital Spoke
item.description: [allAfrica] Rwanda is improving its surgical care with the opening of a newly renovated surgical department at Kibungo Level Two Teaching Hospital, previously known as Kibungo Provincial Referral Hospital - a milestone that brings critical services closer to the community.
